summary: A token refresh race condition triggered repeated retry loops in the authentication API, causing elevated login failures until the retry path was disabled.
customer_impact: A subset of users experienced repeated login prompts and intermittent sign-in failures during the incident window.
root_cause: The retry path allowed concurrent refresh attempts against the same session token, and the system lacked a guard to collapse duplicate refresh work under load.

timeline.event: On-call alerts fired for elevated authentication error rates.
The team linked the spike to repeated token refresh retries during session renewal.
A mitigation disabled the duplicate retry path and error rates began to recover.

action_items.title: Add single-flight protection to token refresh handling.

follow_up_risks: Retry amplification may still appear in adjacent session paths until the broader refresh flow is hardened.
